records.ety: [L. remedium; pref. re- re- + mederi to heal, to cure: cf. F. remde remedy, remdier to remedy. See Medical.]

defs: That which relieves or cures a disease; any medicine or application which puts an end to disease and restores health; -- with for; as, a remedy for the gout.
That which corrects or counteracts an evil of any kind; a corrective; a counteractive; reparation; cure; -- followed by for or against, formerly by to.
quotes: What may else be remedy or cure To evils which our own misdeeds have wrought, He will instruct us.

defs: The legal means to recover a right, or to obtain redress for a wrong.

records.ety: [L. remediare, remediari: cf. F. remdier. See Remedy, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Remedied (-d?d); p. pr. & vb. n. Remedying.]

defs: To apply a remedy to; to relieve; to cure; to heal; to repair; to redress; to correct; to counteract.
quotes: I will remedy this gear ere long.
